manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg

Your secret value function is:

apple: 1, soap: 3, egg: 4

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.

message.content:
I would like the egg and the soap. Can you take the three apples?
Please confirm you will take the three apples.
[Proposal: 1 egg, 1 soap]

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are playing a negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the score you receive.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg

Your secret value function is:

apple: 0, soap: 7, egg: 3

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.


The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.

The other player has submitted a secret proposal; we don't see it? Wait, reading the text: "The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own."

But the other player's proposal hasn't been shown to us. However, in these tasks, presumably the other player's secret proposal is known to us? Actually likely the scenario says they've given a secret proposal; maybe it's in the prior messages but it's missing? Usually for these puzzles, we might have an example. However, the user says "The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own."

We need to figure out what we should propose. We need to maximize our own score given the items and the other player's proposal.

But we don't know the other player's secret proposal. However, perhaps we have to assume that the other player has not yet proposed? The problem is likely that we are supposed to propose what we want, based on our own value function: we value apples at 0, soap at 7, egg at 3. So we want soap and egg. There are 1 soap and 1 egg, both are valuable to us (7 + 3 = 10). So we can propose to take soap and egg. There's also 3 apples, but we value them at 0, so we don't care. However, the other player wants something. Since we don't see what they want, perhaps we need to anticipate complementary proposals: if the other player wants something else (e.g., apples maybe?), but we need to ensure that combined proposals don't exceed available items. So we propose to take soap and egg, leaving apples for the other player.

But we must propose in the format "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]". So we must format our proposal with numbers and object names, separate by commas.

Given our value function: apple 0, soap 7, egg 3. So we propose: "[Proposal: 1 soap, 1 egg]". Could also include apples, but not needed.

But it's unclear if the other player might also want soap or egg. If both proposals conflict (both want soap), then both get zero. So we need to ensure it's complementary. We don't know what the other player wants. But perhaps the other player wants apples (maybe they value apples high). So we propose to take the items we value most: soap and egg. That's probably the best.

But perhaps we need to anticipate that the other player likely wants apples as they are the only other items. The total items: 3 apples, 1 soap, 1 egg. Our value: soap 7, egg 3. The other player's value function unknown. But they likely want apples. The problem perhaps expects that we propose for ourselves the highest valued items: soap and egg. That's the typical solution: propose the items we value most. Since they have the secret proposal already, but we are not told what they want. Possibly they want apples, but we don't need to know. So propose accordingly.

We must use the format with square brackets. Also note that we are limited to maximum of 5 messages each; but this is our proposal, so that's allowed.

Thus final answer: "[Proposal: 1 soap, 1 egg]".

But we could also propose "3 apples"? That would give us 0 points; not helpful. So we propose soap and egg.

Thus answer is just that. However, note that "apple" in the set is plural "apples", "soap" is singular, "egg" is singular. Use "soap" and "egg". The format is "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]". So include the numbers and names.

Thus final output: "[Proposal: 1 soap, 1 egg]".
